title: Mildly Sweet Chinese Vegetable Fried Rice
description: A quick-fix Chinese dish featuring coco sugar and mixed vegetables, perfect for a springy meal, this recipe is also sugar-free-friendly by using coco sugar as a sweetener.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped scallions and cook until they start to soften.
Add the grated carrots and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the frozen peas and cook until they are thawed.
Push the vegetables to one side of the pan.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the other side of the pan.
Pour the beaten eggs into the pan and scramble them until they are cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the vegetables.
Add the cooked rice to the pan, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir-fry the rice with the vegetables and eggs for 2 minutes.
Add the coco sugar, soy sauce, salt, and pepper to the pan and stir-fry for another minute.
